items.title: I blocked location permissions on every site but websites were still tracking me through 3 other signals I hadn't considered

items.title: I tried the most minimal browser I could find and it changed how I work
items.summary: For the longest time, more features have been the strongest motivation for finding new web browsers. It's the same reason I shifted from the likes of Google Chrome and Microsoft Edge to options like Arc and Zen Browser .

items.title: Less than 2% of people use this browser — everyone else is missing out
items.summary: Brave may be a popular beast among power users, but if you look at the overall market share, it’s still a tiny player. According to Statcounter , it only accounts for around 1.35% market share. But after using Brave for

items.title: Android apps track you by default — this free app tells you how bad it really is
items.summary: I know that the apps on my smartphone are full of trackers. It's an unfortunate part of modern life that most of what we do online or otherwise feeds straight into data collection for big business. Then I found a free An

items.title: I dropped Firefox for a fork that ships everything I used to configure manually
items.summary: Enhanced privacy is probably the most common reason people switch to Mozilla Firefox! For what it's worth, Firefox does offer several privacy-centric options that the likes of Google Chrome never will. You'd start notici

items.title: Firefox is better than Chrome, but this third option is miles ahead
items.summary: I've long believed that Mozilla Firefox is a better option than Google Chrome. This is an informed understanding based on Firefox's impressive balance of performance, privacy, and additional features.

items.title: I carry my browser on a flash drive and all my extensions come with me

items.title: I tried every major Android browser, and the one I kept wasn't Chrome, Firefox, or Opera
items.summary: I keep switching browsers on my PC. Though I've settled mostly on Microsoft Edge and Perplexity's Comet there, on my phone, I stuck to Chrome for the longest time. It comes pre-installed and works fine out of the box. Ex

items.title: The safest password is the one you never type
items.summary: Passwords are a mess, and they always have been. I use a password manager, and it makes the whole having to remember 1,400 logins much easier, but even then, it could be easier.
